results.caption:
They didn't build a virus. They built a cartel.

December 6, 2023 — a post appears on BreachForums. No fanfare. No warning. Just a name: DragonForce.

Within 18 months:
🔴 363 confirmed victims
🔴 30+ countries
🔴 212.5% surge in attacks
🔴 £300M+ impact on UK retail alone

This isn't a hacker. It's a franchise.

Affiliates keep 80%. The cartel takes 20%. Everyone wins — except the victim.

The circuit dragon is awake. Is your organization on its list?

#DragonForce #Ransomware #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence #RaaS
results.hook: A mythologized threat actor portrait transforms abstract malware into a visceral, recognizable villain — triggering fear and fascination simultaneously.

results.topic: The Binary Signature — Ransom Note Decoded
results.caption:
The file is called READ_IT.txt

You open it. The world stops.

DragonForce doesn't just encrypt your data — it does it on Windows, Linux, AND ESXi simultaneously using ChaCha8 encryption. Before you even noticed, truesight.sys and rentdrv2.sys had already killed your EDR.

You were blind. They were inside.

ATT&CK techniques on file:
⚠️ T1486 — Data Encrypted for Impact
⚠️ T1562.001 — Disable or Modify Tools (BYOVD)
⚠️ T1071 — Application Layer Protocol C2
⚠️ T1657 — Financial Theft

The ransom note is just paperwork. The real damage was done days ago.

Know the kill chain before they run it.

#MITREATTACK #Ransomware #DragonForce #BYOVD #IncidentResponse
results.hook: Recreating the actual moment a victim opens the ransom note makes abstract threat intelligence viscerally personal — every security professional sees their own screen in this image.

results.caption:
Nobody knows who they really are.

DragonForce publicly emerged on BreachForums on December 6, 2023. By March 19, 2025, they declared themselves a full cartel — absorbing RansomHub affiliates on April 8, 2025.

The attribution map is deliberately blurred:
🌐 Claimed Malaysian hacktivist roots — disputed
🌐 Operational English fluency suggesting Western exposure
🌐 Collaborated with Scattered Spider — a known English-speaking threat group
🌐 RaaS infrastructure complexity suggests nation-state adjacent capability

They want you confused. Confusion buys time.

What we know for certain:
✅ 363 victims confirmed
✅ 52% of victims in the United States
✅ £300M+ impact in UK retail in a single summer

The dragon has no confirmed lair. That's the point.

#ThreatAttribution #DragonForce #CyberEspionage #RansomwareCartel #CyberThreatIntel
results.hook: Geopolitical ambiguity rendered as visual mystery forces the audience to lean in — the 'who is behind this?' question is the most engaging in all of threat intelligence.
